English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University English Department records
Cornell University. English Department
Gilbert, Sandra M.
Abrams, M. H. (Meyer Howard), 1912-2015.
14-12-1227
1 cubic feet. 1 cubic feet.
1891-1987. Correspondence, manuscripts, publications Records documenting the administration of the Cornell English Department. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 522 ======= 522 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2072700
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Evan Earle
Date completed:
September 2015
EAD encoding:
Evan Earle, September 2015
Date modified:
RMC Staff, August 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Records include office, committee, and personnel files; memos; student records; examinations, including an 1891 entrance examination; English scholarship and fellowship materials including lists of winners; selected records of the Freshman Humanities Program; correspondence; pamphlets; minutes of faculty meetings (1946-1957); and records of the department chairman, 1948-1968.
English Department lectures presented Monday, January 29, 2007 at Hollis E. Cornell Auditorium, Goldwin Smith Hall, Cornell University: Sandra M. Gilbert, "Finding Atlantis: Thirty Years of Exploring Women's Literary Traditions" and M.H. Abrams, "On Reading Poetry Aloud."
Cite As:
Cornell University English Department records, #14-12-1227. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to departmental records restricted to the permission of the office of origin.
Oral interpretation of poetry. Women and literature. Feminism and literature. Cornell University -- : Entrance requirements -- : English. Cornell University -- : Examinations -- : English. Cornell University -- : Funds and scholarships. Cornell University -- : Prizes. Cornell University -- : Students. Cornell University. Freshman Humanities Program Cornell University. English Department
